skill.name: software-architecture
skill.description: Guide software design with Clean Architecture, SOLID principles, domain naming, and practical review checklists.
skill.summary: Apply Clean Architecture and SOLID principles to design and review maintainable software systems.
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, a frontmatter description, and a section heading as possible security issues. Manual review found no executable code, no shell execution, no cryptography implementation, no reconnaissance command, and no prompt injection attempt.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings are Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and directory diagrams. They do not instruct the agent to run shell commands or execute untrusted input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged syntax is visible as Markdown documentation and TypeScript illustration. No executable command, script runner, or command interpolation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography finding points to the frontmatter description. That line describes software architecture guidance and contains no cryptographic algorithm, library call, or security implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text for the skill description. There is no evidence of DES, MD5, SHA1, or any cryptographic operation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Pattern
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance finding points to an anti-patterns section heading. The surrounding content lists design anti-patterns and does not request system inspection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a documentation heading. No command such as uname, whoami, environment inspection, or host enumeration appears there.

content.value_statement: Software teams often struggle to keep responsibilities, dependencies, and naming consistent. This skill provides practical architecture rules, review prompts, and checklists for maintainable systems.
actual_capabilities: Explains SOLID principles with simple responsibility and dependency guidance.
Maps code into Clean Architecture layers and dependency direction.
Suggests early return patterns to reduce nested conditional logic.
Defines practical function, component, and file size limits.
Encourages domain-specific naming and separation of concerns.
Provides a checklist for new code and architecture review.
limitations: It is guidance only and does not analyze repositories automatically.
It does not include framework-specific architecture templates.
It does not validate runtime performance or production behavior.
Some examples use Korean explanations, so teams may translate them.

use_cases.title: Review a Service Design
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to evaluate layer boundaries, dependency direction, and responsibility splits before implementation.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Growing Module
use_cases.description: Identify God classes, deep nesting, generic names, and mixed UI or business logic responsibilities.

use_cases.title: Teach Architecture Basics
use_cases.description: Introduce SOLID, Clean Architecture layers, and separation of concerns with concise examples.

prompt_templates.title: Check Basic SOLID Fit
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a small class or module
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this class using SOLID principles. Focus on single responsibility, dependency direction, and interface size. Give three practical changes.

prompt_templates.title: Map Layers
prompt_templates.prompt: Map these files into domain, application, infrastructure, and presentation layers. Point out dependencies that face the wrong direction.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Refactor
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a refactoring plan for this module. Reduce deep nesting, separate concerns, improve naming, and keep behavior unchanged.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Architecture Decision
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this architecture decision against Clean Architecture, DDD boundaries, testability, and library-first thinking. Include risks and alternatives.

output_examples.input: Review a user service that validates input, calls an API, stores records, and formats UI labels.
output: The service mixes application, infrastructure, and presentation concerns.
Move UI label formatting to the presentation layer.
Create a repository or gateway interface for storage and API access.
Keep validation in the use case or domain layer based on business ownership.

output_examples.input: Check whether a new payment module follows Clean Architecture.
output: Define payment entities in the domain layer.
Place payment orchestration in application use cases.
Keep gateway SDK calls in infrastructure adapters.
Expose controller or UI logic only through the presentation layer.

output_examples.input: Improve naming for utility files in an order workflow.
output: Replace generic names with domain terms such as OrderCalculator or DiscountPolicy.
Group files by workflow responsibility instead of a common helper folder.
Use names that describe business intent, not implementation convenience.

best_practices: Start architecture reviews by identifying responsibilities and ownership boundaries.
Keep dependencies pointing inward from frameworks toward domain rules.
Prefer existing libraries or platform services before adding custom infrastructure.
anti_patterns: Using generic helper folders that hide domain intent.
Mixing UI, API access, and business rules in one component.
Creating large classes or files that own unrelated responsibilities.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps design and review software structure using Clean Architecture, SOLID principles, naming rules, and practical checklists.

faq.question: Can it review existing code?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide relevant files or a design summary, and ask for layer, dependency, and responsibility feedback.

faq.question: Does it generate complete application scaffolds?
faq.answer: No. It gives architecture guidance and review structure, but it does not provide a full framework scaffold.

faq.question: Is it tied to one programming language?
faq.answer: No. The examples reference TypeScript, but the architecture principles apply across many languages.

faq.question: How should teams use it in code review?
faq.answer: Use it to check responsibility boundaries, dependency direction, naming clarity, file size, and testable structure.

faq.question: Does it require network or command execution?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance and does not require network access or shell commands.
